mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals the onset of a monetary easing cycle that lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as liquidity conditions improve.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.02% one month prior with market pricing imminent cuts ahead of Sept 2007 FOMC meeting
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and forward 20d returns remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or returns following rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in housing starts below a critical threshold signals a deepening residential investment collapse that propagates through construction employment, consumer wealth effects and credit markets, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST level falls below 1,400 thousand units, representing a decline of more than 30% from its prior cyclical peak
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1381.0 thousand units, sharply below 2005-2006 peak levels above 2000
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where HOUST triggers the threshold, or Rank IC stays below 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment growth decelerates simultaneously with a rising unemployment rate, the joint signal indicates labor market turning point that precedes broader economic contraction, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days beyond what either series predicts alone.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth falls below 1% while UNRATE rises for two consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.6% with PAYEMS growth showing signs of moderation into mid-2007
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) relative to additive single-series model, or joint-trigger episodes show positive median forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month momentum in industrial production reflects continued factory-sector expansion and corporate earnings resilience, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as short-term risk sentiment tracks near-term output data.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 114.4254 one month prior, near cycle highs
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO month-over-month change and forward 5-day returns remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate cuts occurring while housing starts continue to decline signal that the Fed is reacting defensively to an unfolding credit and housing crisis rather than proactively stimulating growth; in this regime, rate cuts fail to offset the negative wealth and credit effects of the housing bust, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines by more than 25bp within a quarter while HOUST simultaneously falls more than 5% over the same period
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.02% amid expectations of cuts, HOUST at 1381.0 continuing multi-year decline
regime_hint: late-cycle credit stress with Fed behind the curve
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of joint-trigger episodes, or the interaction term is dominated by the single-series FEDFUNDS effect in regression decomposition.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate that crosses above its trailing 12-month low by a meaningful margin signals the start of labor market deterioration that historically precedes recessions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as markets price in earnings downgrades.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points above its trailing 12-month low
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.6%, near cycle lows but showing early signs of stabilization/uptick risk
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following UNRATE trigger are not significantly negative (p > 0.05) relative to unconditional baseline over a 3-year rolling evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: A slowdown in retail sales growth reflects weakening consumer spending, which constitutes roughly two-thirds of GDP, and signals softening corporate revenue growth ahead, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 377,615 million dollars one month prior, with growth pace under scrutiny amid credit tightening
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS 3-month growth rate and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth combined with still-elevated policy rates indicates the economy is operating near capacity constraints that the Fed has not yet eased, creating a regime where positive supply-side momentum is offset by tight monetary conditions, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as tightening effects dominate.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO year-over-year growth exceeds 2% while FEDFUNDS remains above 5%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.02% with INDPRO at 114.4254 reflecting still-restrictive policy stance
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with resilient output
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes show positive median 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of cases, or interaction coefficient loses significance (p > 0.10) relative to single-factor FEDFUNDS model.
